page: Microsoft Azure DS2 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DS server family. It is powered by an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The instance features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs, with 1.0 thread per core. Memory allocation consists of 7.0 GB of RAM, translating to 3.5 GB per core. For local storage, the instance includes a bundled 14 GB storage volume. The underlying virtualization is managed via Microsoft Hyper-V.

Benchmark evaluations place the performance of this instance in the lower tiers. In stress-ng CPU tests, both single-core and multi-core performance fall into the bottom 25% category. Similarly, memory bandwidth tests for both small cached blocks and larger uncached blocks register in the bottom 25%. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench multi-core scores are categorized in the bottom 10%, reflecting limited parallel processing capabilities. Cryptographic performance, as measured by the PassMark CPU encryption test, also ranks in the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the DS2 offers an entry-level dedicated hardware profile with a balanced resource density of 3.5 GB of RAM per vCPU. However, the low benchmark-per-resource tradeoffs make it less suitable for compute-intensive or memory-heavy workloads. It is best suited for low-intensity general-purpose workloads, basic static web serving, minor database operations, or lightweight development and testing environments that do not require high-throughput processing.
description: Microsoft Azure DS2 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DS family, configured with an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. It provides 2 dedicated physical cores (2 vCPUs), 7.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 14 GB local storage volume, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Performance benchmarks indicate that the instance resides in the lower tiers, with stress-ng CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and Geekbench multi-core scores ranking in the bottom 10% to 25%. This hardware profile offers an entry-level dedicated resource allocation, making it a qualitatively cost-effective option for low-demand applications. The DS2 is best suited for lightweight workloads such as basic static web serving, minor database operations, and development or testing environments that do not require high-performance computing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DS2 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 7.0 GB of RAM, and 14 GB of local storage, designed for low-intensity workloads.

tagline: An entry-level dedicated virtual machine with balanced memory-to-core allocation for low-intensity general-purpose workloads and basic development environments.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs
7.0 GB of system memory and 14 GB of local storage
Built on x86_64 architecture and Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor
Positioned in the bottom 25% for CPU and memory bandwidth benchmarks
Best suited for static web serving and lightweight development environments
